name: Malpasillo Bridge and Estrechura del Pusa Loop
name_original: Santa Ana de Pusa – circular al puente de Malpasillo por la Estrechura del Pusa
description: Enjoy an easy 2.8-mile (4.6 km) hike in Santa Ana de Pusa, featuring the scenic Estrechura del Pusa gorge and the historic Malpasillo Bridge
region_intro: Hiking around Santa Ana De Pusa offers access to diverse natural landscapes within the Montes de Toledo and the scenic Pusa valley. The region features rugged terrain with granite formations, abundant holm oaks, and various scrublands. The Pusa River is a central feature, flowing through the area and forming the Gorges of the Pusa River, which are accessible via several trails. This varied environment provides a range of hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Santa Ana De Pusa The most popular hiking route is Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular, a 5.3 miles (8.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Pusa River and surrounding natural features. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sangrera River Valley Trail, a moderate 10.4 miles (16.7 km) path. This trail provides extensive views of the river valley and its varied terrain. Local hikers also love the Malpasillo Bridge and Estrechura del Pusa Loop, a 2.8 miles (4.6 km) trail leading through a narrow section of the Pusa River, often completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Hiking around Santa Ana De Pusa is defined by river paths, granite formations, and holm oak forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to moderate excursions. The routes in Santa Ana De Pusa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 2 reviews. More than 8 hikers have used komoot to explore Santa Ana De Pusa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: Santa Ana de Pusa offers a selection of 4 hiking routes, ranging from easy to moderate difficulty. These trails allow you to explore the diverse landscapes of the Pusa valley and the Montes de Toledo.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when hiking in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: The region is characterized by rugged terrain with granite formations, abundant holm oaks (encinas), and various scrublands. Many trails follow the picturesque Pusa River, offering a mix of riverside paths and woodland sections.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are easy hiking options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021127098/malpasillo-bridge-and-estrechura-del-pusa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Malpasillo Bridge and Estrechura del Pusa Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.6 km trail that takes about 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14706835" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabonero Dam – Estrechura of the Pusa River loop from Santa Ana de Pusa&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 6.9 km loop starting directly from the town.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: Yes, several routes in the area are circular. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1219443515/rio-pusa-from-los-navalucillos-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Río Pusa from Los Navalucillos — circular&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1021134760/presa-del-jabonero-circular-from-retamoso-de-la-jara-via-the-pusa-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Presa del Jabonero – circular from Retamoso de la Jara via the Pusa river&lt;/a&gt; also offers a moderate circular experience.

Q: What are the best times of year to go hiking in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for hiking in Santa Ana de Pusa. During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant, with blooming wildflowers in spring and rich colors in autumn. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if hiking during that time.

Q: Can I see any historical or natural landmarks while hiking?
A: Yes, the area features notable landmarks. The Pusa River itself is a central natural feature, with dramatic sections like the 'Hoces del Río Pusa' (Gorges of the Pusa River). You can also visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5421745" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jabonero Dam&lt;/a&gt;, an old weir on the Pusa River that once powered local mills and is integrated into several hiking paths.

Q: Are the trails in Santa Ana de Pusa suitable for dogs?
A: Many trails in natural areas like Santa Ana de Pusa are generally dog-friendly, but it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Ensure you carry enough water for your pet, particularly on longer routes or during warmer months, as water sources may be limited.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Hikers often praise the serene river paths, the unique granite formations, and the immersive experience of walking through holm oak forests.

Q: Are there any trails that offer views of the Pusa River?
A: Absolutely. The Pusa River is a defining feature of the region, and many trails follow its course. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1218737658/sangrera-river-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sangrera River Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, provides extensive views of the river valley and its varied terrain.

Q: What should I bring for a day hike in Santa Ana de Pusa?
A: For a day hike, it's recommended to bring sufficient water, snacks, appropriate footwear for varied terrain, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a map or GPS device. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are also advisable due to potential temperature changes.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available?
A: While there are no routes classified as 'difficult' in our current selection, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1218737658/sangrera-river-valley-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sangrera River Valley Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 16.7 km path that offers a longer excursion, taking approximately 4 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route provides a more extensive exploration of the river valley.
tips: Built in the 19th century to cross the course of the Pusa River, the bridge named Malpasillo is neoclassical in style and stands out in the picturesque landscape that surrounds it. It owes its name to the small and narrow rocky gorge where it sits, since it may seem like a jump but it is undoubtedly a "bad step" due to the danger it represents.

This area of the Pusa river known as the "Narrowness" has been used in the past with mills that, although they are currently in ruins, I recommend visiting them since the landscape is very picturesque.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete the Santa Ana de Pusa – circular to the Malpasillo bridge via the Estrechura del Pusa?
faqs.answer: This circular route typically takes around 1 hour and 15 minutes to complete, making it a pleasant and accessible hike.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is considered an "easy walk" (caminata fácil) and is suitable for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. Its short distance and minimal elevation gain make it very accessible.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting and ending in Santa Ana de Pusa. While specific parking details aren't provided, as a circular route from the town, parking would typically be available within or on the outskirts of Santa Ana de Pusa itself.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route features two significant highlights: the Malpasillo Bridge, a 19th-century neoclassical structure spanning the Pusa River, and the picturesque Estrechura of the Pusa River, a narrow rocky gorge with historical ruins of old mills.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally easy, characterized by rugged landscapes with granite formations, holm oaks, and various shrubs. The path follows the Pusa River, particularly through the narrow rocky gorge of "la Estrechura".

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is generally considered dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through areas with wildlife or other hikers.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Given the description of the terrain and the presence of a river, spring and autumn are likely the most pleasant seasons for hiking, offering mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer might be hot, and winter could be chilly, but the trail is accessible year-round.

faqs.question: Are permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Puente de Malpasillo, Camino de la zarza, Paseo del Prado, and Camino del Andariego.

faqs.question: Can I reach the trailhead using public transport?
faqs.answer: While Santa Ana de Pusa is a small town, public transport options might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules to Santa Ana de Pusa, as direct routes to the trailhead itself are unlikely.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Pusa River?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque scenery, especially within the "la Estrechura" section, where the river flows through a narrow rocky gorge. The surrounding landscape features granite formations and Mediterranean vegetation like holm oaks and shrubs.
